article_title: Former Twitter CEO Parag Agrawal Doubles Parallel's Valuation to $2 Billion in Five Months as Sequoia Bets on Web Infrastructure for AI Agents

content_preview.title: Former Twitter CEO Parag Agrawal Doubles Parallel's Valuation to $2 Billion in Five Months as Sequoia Bets on Web Infrastructure for AI Agents
content_preview.summary: Parallel Web Systems closed a $100 million Series B led by Sequoia Capital at a $2 billion valuation, more than doubling the startup's worth since its November Series A and bringing total funding to $230 million.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Parallel Web Systems, the AI infrastructure startup founded by former Twitter chief executive Parag Agrawal, has closed a $100 million Series B round led by Sequoia Capital at a $2 billion valuation, according to [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/29/parallel-web-systems-hits-2b-valuation-five-months-after-its-last-big-raise/). The deal more than doubles the company's $740 million valuation from its Series A just five months earlier and brings cumulative funding to $230 mil...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Solid funding News piece. All deal mechanics, investor list, and customer names verified verbatim against TechCrunch + SiliconANGLE.

source_verification.https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/29/parall: source-0.html — verified: $100M Series B, Sequoia lead, $2B valuation, $740M prior Series A, Kleiner Perkins/Index/Khosla returning, First Round/Spark/Terrain/Abstract new, Clay/Harvey/Notion/Opendoor customers, 100,000 developers.
source_verification.https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/28/para: source-1.html — verified: Tavily and Exa Labs as competitors, Agrawal 'use the web a lot more than humans' quote, 'deep research' framing, Gabe Pereyra (Harvey co-founder) 'granular control over which websites' quote.
source_verification.https://techfundingnews.com/parag-agrawa: source-2.html — corroborates founding details, provenance-tracking framing, Diffbot competitor mention.

editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. Honest about open questions (revenue undisclosed, 100k developer figure unverified, value-chain accrual question).
editor_notes.originality: Not previously covered. Cross-reference to April 22 Cursor $50B-talk article is appropriate.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Standard funding-round coverage with all specifics verified.
